violations.text: A knife and cup were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment to sanitize in 3 bay sink until serviced and dispensing proper concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen and basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Please email proof of enrollment in a food safety course to inspector. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023
Single-service items (straws) were observed stored on the floor in the dry storage area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023
The dishwasher located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Per person in charge, establishment does not use the dishwasher in the kitchen. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023

The floors and walls throughout the facility (kitchen and basement) have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Per person in charge, establishment has a cfsm. Per person in charge, establishment only cooks on the weekend. Establishment could not provide a certificate during time of inspection. Please provide proof of cfsm or enroll an employee in a food safety course. Please email inspector proof of certification or proof of enrollment in a course. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023

Equipment was observed in the hand-washing sink in the kitchen and bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection (see attached disposal form). Ice machine must be cleaned. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023

Potato salad was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheitv in the beer walk-in cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the walk-in for more than 4 hours. Potato salad was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). The ambient air temperature was observed at 47 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Establishment must get walk-in cooler repaired/adjusted/replaced if storing time/temperature for control for safety foods. Per person in charge, walk-in cooler will only be used to store alcohol. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023

Food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023
Beverages were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023
Single-service articles (straws and silverware) were observed stored on the floor. Single-service articles must be stored at least 6 inches off the ground. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023

The dishwasher located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Per person in charge, establishment does not use the dishwasher in the kitchen, they use the bar dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023
The handwashing sink and ice machine are leaking. Plumbing systems must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023

The floors and walls throughout the facility (kitchen and basement) have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. Water is accumulating on the floor in the basement. Establishment has cardboard on the floor. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023
